matter.title: Divorce of Olivia B. Ellsworth and Maria Q. Fairbanks
matter.summary: Olivia B. Ellsworth petitions to divorce Maria Q. Fairbanks in Cumberland County District Court, citing irreconcilable marital differences, and asks the court to resolve parental rights for three minor child(ren), child support, and division of marital property.

fact_pattern.summary: Olivia B. Ellsworth petitions to divorce Maria Q. Fairbanks in Cumberland County District Court, citing irreconcilable marital differences, and asks the court to resolve parental rights for three minor child(ren), child support, and division of marital property.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Olivia B. Ellsworth and Maria Q. Fairbanks were married on 2010-08-15 in Camden, Maine. The parties have three minor child(ren) together: Ingrid B. Kingsley, Marcus W. Roe and Omar W. Loe. After several years of growing conflict and a period of unsuccessful counseling, the parties separated on 2025-04-20.

Since separation, the children have lived primarily with Maria Q. Fairbanks, while the other parent has exercised weekend contact. The parties jointly own or rent a jointly owned condominium and share several financial accounts. They disagree about a long-term parenting schedule and about how to divide retirement savings accumulated during the marriage.

Olivia has resided in Cumberland County, Maine for approximately 36 months, satisfying the residency requirement to file. Olivia now seeks a divorce on the ground of irreconcilable marital differences, a workable parental rights and responsibilities order, child support consistent with the Maine guidelines, and an equitable division of marital property and debt.
undisputed_facts: The parties were married on 2010-08-15 in Camden, Maine.
The parties have three minor child(ren) of the marriage.
The parties separated on 2025-04-20 and have lived apart since.
disputed_facts: The long-term allocation of parental rights and responsibilities and the primary residence of the children.
The characterization and division of retirement accounts and the equity in the jointly owned condominium.
Whether spousal support is appropriate and, if so, in what amount and for how long.

timeline.event: Olivia and Maria married in Camden, Maine.
Complaint for divorce prepared for filing in Portland District Court.

third_parties.name: Acadia Print Works
third_parties.relevance: Source of income and benefits relevant to support calculations.

third_parties.name: Lighthouse Trust Company
third_parties.relevance: Holds the marital checking, savings, and mortgage accounts.

exchanges.question: What outcome are you hoping for?
exchanges.answer: I want a fair parenting schedule where our children are primarily with me, reasonable child support, and a clean split of what we built together. I am not trying to punish Maria.

exchanges.question: How are the children doing, and what is the current schedule?
exchanges.answer: They are doing okay given everything. They are mostly with Maria Q. Fairbanks during the week and see the other parent on weekends. I want to keep their school and routine stable.

exchanges.question: Tell me about the marital finances.
exchanges.answer: We have a jointly owned condominium, two retirement accounts, a joint checking and savings, and some credit card debt. I do not have full visibility into Maria's retirement balances.

exchanges.question: Are there any safety concerns or history of abuse?
exchanges.answer: No physical abuse. There were some heated arguments, but nothing that needed police involvement.

exchanges.question: Is there any urgency to filing?
exchanges.answer: I would like to get this moving this season so we have orders in place before the next school year.

goals: Obtain a parental rights and responsibilities order with the children's primary residence with Maria Q. Fairbanks.
Establish child support consistent with the Maine child support guidelines.
Achieve an equitable division of marital property and debt.
priorities: Stability and routine for the minor children.
desired_outcomes: Primary residence of the children with Maria Q. Fairbanks and shared parental rights.
Retention of a fair share of retirement savings and home equity.
non_starters: Any arrangement that destabilizes the children's schooling.

constraints.budget: Moderate; willing to mediate but will litigate parenting if needed.
constraints.timeline: Wants temporary orders before the next school year.

issues.issue: Entitlement to and amount of spousal support
sub_questions: Is spousal support appropriate given the marriage length and incomes?

issues.issue: Allocation of parental rights and responsibilities for the minor children
sub_questions: Should parental rights be shared, allocated, or sole?
What primary residence and contact schedule serves the children's best interests?
issues.client_position: Seeks shared parental rights with primary residence with Maria Q. Fairbanks.

sub_questions: Is a fee contribution warranted by the parties' relative resources?

issues.issue: Child support under the Maine guidelines
sub_questions: What are each party's gross incomes?
Who pays for health insurance and child care?
issues.client_position: Seeks guideline child support based on accurate income figures.

issues.issue: Division of marital property and debt
sub_questions: Which assets are marital versus non-marital?
How should retirement accounts and home equity be divided?

expert_opinions.opinion: The lower-earning spouse can return to full-time work within a year.
Both parents are fit; a primary-residence-with-shared-contact schedule best serves the children's stability.
expert_opinions.basis: Clinical interviews, home visits, and collateral contacts.

evidence.title: Retirement account statements
evidence.description: Quarterly statements for both parties' retirement accounts.

evidence.title: Recent pay stubs
evidence.description: Three months of pay stubs for income verification.

evidence.title: Plaintiff's financial statement (FM-050)
evidence.description: Court financial statement of income, expenses, assets, and debts.

amounts.label: Estimated marital home equity
amounts.basis: Estimated fair market value less mortgage.
